b: AKS + KEDA (scaling) + Dapr (building blocks) + Envoy (ingress), fully abstracted — no K8s API access.
ACA: serverless, no cluster ops, no CRDs/operators. AKS: full K8s control, custom operators, meshes, node access.

f: ACA vs App Service vs ACI vs Functions
b: ACA: microservices/containers. App Service: web apps + slots. ACI: single simple containers. Functions: event-driven code with bindings.
Isolation boundary sharing VNet + Log Analytics; internal (private ingress) or external.
Immutable app snapshots; traffic splitting across revisions = canary/blue-green.
KEDA-based: HTTP concurrency, CPU/memory, Service Bus/Event Hubs/Kafka/cron scalers; minReplicas 0 = scale to zero.
Run-to-completion workloads: manual, scheduled (cron), or event-driven (KEDA).
Mix Consumption (serverless) and Dedicated (fixed vCPU/mem, GPU) in one environment; enables UDR/firewall egress control.
Sidecar per app: service invocation with mTLS/retries, pub/sub, state stores, bindings, secrets — opt-in per app.
Managed identity + AcrPull role — never admin credentials.
Key Vault references resolved via managed identity.
Set minReplicas ≥ 1 for latency-sensitive APIs; scale to zero for batch/event workloads.
OAuth = authorization (access tokens). OIDC = authentication layer on top (ID tokens).
ID token (client authenticates user), access token (sent to APIs), refresh token (silent renewal).
Authorization code + PKCE (implicit flow is deprecated).
Client credentials — prefer certificate, managed identity, or workload identity federation over client secrets.

f: Flow: API calling downstream API as the user
b: On-Behalf-Of (OBO) token exchange.
Device code flow.
Legacy username/password flow — breaks MFA/Conditional Access; avoid.
Registration = global app definition (home tenant). Service principal/enterprise app = its identity instance per tenant.
scp = delegated scopes (user context). roles = app roles (app-only tokens or user role assignments).
Application (app-only) permissions ALWAYS require admin consent.
Signature (JWKS), issuer, audience, expiry/nbf → then authorize on scp/roles. Every hop validates (zero trust).
validate-jwt / validate-azure-ad-token with required-claims; reject at the edge, backend re-validates.
CIAM (successor to Azure AD B2C): consumer sign-up/sign-in, social IdPs, custom branding.
B2B = partner guests in YOUR tenant. External ID/CIAM = customers in a separate consumer directory.
If-then policy engine on sign-in signals (user, device, location, risk) → require MFA/compliant device/block.
Azure-managed Entra identity for resources — zero secrets for Azure-to-Azure auth.
Time-boxed, scoped delegation (e.g., blob download links); prefer user-delegation SAS; otherwise use Entra RBAC.
Mutual certificate auth for high-assurance service-to-service (APIM client certs to backend), layered with JWT + private network.
Verify explicitly. Least privilege (JIT/JEA). Assume breach (segment, encrypt, monitor).
Issuer varies per tenant — validate iss/tid against an allowlist or pattern.
Single entry point handling auth, throttling, routing, transformation — APIM's role.
Separate tailored backend per client type (mobile/web/partner).
Route slices of a monolith to new services via a facade until the monolith is gone.
Offload cross-cutting concerns / direct requests / compose multiple backend calls into one response.
Trip after repeated failures, fail fast, probe recovery — prevents cascading failure. Pair with retry+backoff.
Partition resource pools so one consumer/workload can't exhaust capacity for others.
Exponential backoff + jitter, idempotent operations only, cap attempts, honor Retry-After.
Separate command (write) and query (read) models — scale and optimize independently.
Append-only event log as source of truth; state rebuilt by replay; projections serve reads.
Check cache → miss → read store → populate cache; invalidate on writes.
Distributed transaction via local transactions + compensating actions (choreography or orchestration).
Reference message + payload in blob storage — beats message size limits.
Co-located helper container for connectivity, security, observability (e.g., Dapr).
Translation layer isolating a new system from legacy models/protocols.
Stamps: repeatable isolated stack units per tenant/region. Geode: active-active regional nodes behind global routing.
Expose functional health probes for LBs/orchestrators to gate traffic.
Broker buffer between spiky producers and steady consumers.
Reject/degrade excess load to protect the service (APIM rate-limit/quota).
Identity/governance/monitoring 25–30% • Data storage 25–30% • Business continuity 10–15% • Infrastructure 30–35%.
~50 questions incl. case studies, 700/1000 to pass, ~$165; Expert cert requires AZ-104 prerequisite.
Multiply: 99.95% × 99.9% ≈ 99.85%. Every serial dependency lowers availability.
1 − (1−A)²: two 99.9% regions ≈ 99.9999% theoretical.
RTO = max downtime tolerated. RPO = max data loss window tolerated.
Reliability, Security, Cost Optimization, Operational Excellence, Performance Efficiency.
Strategy → Plan → Ready (landing zones) → Adopt (Migrate/Innovate) + ongoing Govern & Manage.
Map stated requirements to the CHEAPEST compliant service; never gold-plate; watch 'minimize cost/effort' qualifiers.
Zones survive datacenter failure (same region, low latency). Region pairs/multi-region survive regional disasters.
Password hash sync (simplest, recommended) → pass-through auth → ADFS federation (most infra, most control).
Pricing Calculator (pre-deploy), TCO Calculator (on-prem compare), Cost Management (actuals), Advisor (savings recs), Reservations/Savings Plans (commit discounts).
Azure Monitor metrics + Log Analytics (KQL) + App Insights (APM) + alerts/action groups + workbooks.
Microsoft Learn Practice Assessment: learn.microsoft.com/credentials/certifications/exams/az-305/practice/assessment
MS Learn AZ-305 paths, John Savill's AZ-305 playlist (YouTube), MeasureUp official practice tests, Tutorials Dojo/Whizlabs mocks, Architecture Center case studies.

f: Architecture Center + WAF role in exam
b: AZ-305 explicitly aligns to Well-Architected Framework and Cloud Adoption Framework — justify every recommendation with them.
Developer APIM tier in prod (no SLA) • Geo-DR replicates metadata only • service endpoint ≠ private endpoint • NotActions ≠ deny • scp ≠ roles.
Front Door+WAF → internal APIM → ACA/AKS (managed identities) → Service Bus → workers → private-endpoint data tier; hub VNet with firewall/ER; Key Vault + Log Analytics throughout.
